visual_description:
A medium close-up shot captures a conversation between a man and a woman in a bustling, crowded cafeteria. The camera is positioned over the woman's shoulder, focusing on the man. The man, with dark hair and a beard, wears a striped sweater and holds a piece of food, gesturing with it as he speaks. The woman, with voluminous, wavy blonde hair, is seen from the back, her attention fixed on him. The background is filled with the indistinct chatter and murmur of many people talking at once, creating the ambient sound of a busy cafeteria or dining hall. A man speaks in a calm, slightly defensive tone, making a general observation. "I don't hear anyone complaining."

The camera angle reverses, now showing the woman from the front and the man from over his shoulder. The woman, now in focus, has a surprised expression. She wears a dark sweater over a white collared shirt and holds a sandwich in her hands, examining it as she listens. A woman replies quickly and dismissively, her voice sharp and a bit condescending. "Of course not, you're out the door too fast."

The shot returns to the initial over-the-shoulder view, focusing again on the man. He continues to speak
